input:
RE-HOSPITALIZATION REPORT
This is the child Bouteldja Mohamed El Amir aged 04 years old from Mascara and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, monitored at our level since 06/30/2025 for hyper IgE type syndromic immunodeficiency admitted today for his 05th treatment of venoglobulins
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: hyper IgE type immunodeficiency. Associated diagnosis: epilepsy with neurodevelopmental disorder under treatment. Age of diagnosis: 03 years and 7 months
Date of diagnosis: 02/06/2025. The diagnosis of primary immunodeficiency type hyper IgE is suspected based on the following criteria:
Anamnestics:
A death at a young age in the family. Family history of primary immunodeficiency. CLINIC:
Chronic fungal skin infections from the age of 9 months such as onychomycosis. A personal and family atopy. Paraclinic:
Assessment of the immunodeficiency carried out on 02/06/2025 (attached) initial finding in favor of a suspicion of an immunodeficiency type reduction of Th17 with gain of function of the STAT 1 gene. (To be completed by a phosphorylation test of the STAT 1 gene)
IgG: 6.99 g/l IgA: 0.34 g/l IgM: 0.57 g/l
IgE: 390 IU/ml (high)
TH 17: 0.23% (decreased). The STAT 1 phosphorylation test: done on: 07/21/2025 (attached) returning: correct phosphorylation of the STAT 1 gene. The STAT 3 phosphorylation test done on: 07/27/2025 (attached): returning in favor of correct STAT 3 phosphorylation (does not eliminate the diagnosis of hyper IgE syndrome). Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress:
Depakine:20 mg/kg/day. Kepra:25 mg/kg/day. Bactrim: 10 mg/kg/day. Itraconazole: 10 mg/kg/day. Events since the last visit: 10/12/2025:
Consultations: No
On the respiratory level: without abnormalities. On the digestive level: preserved transit, preserved appetite. On the skin: No fungal lesions of onychomycosis with disappearance of generalized depigmentation of the skin accentuated on the 2 upper limbs for 5 days (a dermatological opinion was requested but not done)
Neurologically: (language and behavior): improvement in the child's language (he says words and sentences in a coherent way), with a reduction in hyperactivity. Reason for hospitalization: admitted today for 5th treatment of venoglobulins. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 15.5 kg (-0.41 DS)
Size: 98 cm (-0.86 DS)
PC: 49 cm (-1.33 DS)
BMI: 16.1 (+0.61 SD)
Gomez: 97% (normal nutritional status). General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 36.1 C, integuments and conjunctiva normal-colored. Head and neck examination: broad nasal base, small left cervical lymphadenopathy
ENT examination: throat: clean, no rhinorrhea or otorrhea. Examination of the oral cavity: absence of thrush or dental abscess. Pleuropulmonary examination:
Absence of thoracic deformation. Eupneic FR: 24 cycles / min. clear auscultation in both pulmonary fields. SpO2: 99% under ambient air. Cardiovascular examination: B1 B2 clearly audible, no breathing or added noises. HR: 103 beats/min. Abdominopelvic examination: absence of scar or collateral circulation. Soft abdomen, absence of organomegaly, preserved transit. Genital examination: male morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation. Neurological examination: conscious, cooperating child, absence of convulsions, improvement in language (he says words in sentences in a coherent manner), with regression of motor hyperactivity. Osteoarticular examination: ligamentous hyperlaxity, no skeletal abnormalities
Examination of the skin and appendages: No onychomycotic fungal lesions, presence of some healing lesions on the upper and lower limbs. The rest of the clinical examination is without abnormalities. Paraclinical examinations: (requested):
As part of monitoring the side effects of the therapy, an assessment was requested:
CBC: GB: 10800 (PNN: 4280, LYMPH: 4800, EOSINO: 630); Hb: 12.4 g/dl;
PLQ: 225000
TP: 100%
UREA: 0.36 CREAT: 5.79
TGO: 35.13 TGP: 20.16
Residual IgG level: 8.26 IU/ml
Coproparasitology + dermatology opinion requested: not done
CAT:
Hospitalization in day hospital
Conditioning
Infusion of multivalent immunoglobulins No.: 0.6g/kg or 10 g
Hygiene-dietary measures (therapeutic education, notebook keeping)
Close monitoring
The child did not present any side effects (no headaches, no hypo or high blood pressure, no allergic reaction). at the end of the treatment, we noticed a leak of half of the infusion. The mother was offered to take back the leaked quantity but she refused and left against medical advice.
On 11/18/2025, the mother returned in the morning to finish the rest of the quantity, namely 05g, which passed without incident
Discharged today after end of treatment with prophylactic treatment (Bactrim and Itraconazole) with appointment for the 6th treatment
The exams requested:
Copro parasitology of stools (bacteriological map). Request an updated report on follow-up in Neuropediatrics.
